tasks.title: Deploy to Vercel environment (DEFERRED)
tasks.description: Verify CI changes, check application works locally, then deploy frontend to Vercel and backend to Render/Railway production environment. DEFERRED: Will be done after application is feature-complete.
tasks.details:
1. Review and verify recent CI changes:
   - Check .github/workflows/ci.yml for any recent modifications
   - Verify all security scripts are working correctly
   - Confirm pre-commit hooks are functioning
2. Local validation:
   - Run backend locally: 'python -m uvicorn api.main:app --reload'
   - Run frontend locally: 'cd apps/web && npm run dev'
   - Test key functionality (chat API, property search)
   - Run security scan: 'python scripts/security/local_scan.py'
   - Run tests: 'npm run test'
3. Prepare for Vercel deployment (FRONTEND):
   - Update environment variables for Vercel
   - Configure BACKEND_API_URL in Vercel dashboard
   - Set up production API key
4. Deploy frontend to Vercel:
   - Connect GitHub repository to Vercel
   - Configure build settings (root directory: apps/web, build command: npm run build)
   - Set environment variables in Vercel dashboard
   - Deploy and verify deployment is accessible
5. Deploy backend to Render/Railway (BACKEND):
   - Create new Web Service on Render or Railway
   - Connect GitHub repository
   - Configure build command: pip install -e .[dev]
   - Configure start command: python -m uvicorn api.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT
   - Set environment variables (API keys, CORS, etc.)
   - Deploy and verify API health endpoint
6. Post-deployment validation:
   - Test live frontend at Vercel URL
   - Test live backend at Render/Railway URL
   - Monitor logs for errors
   - Verify API connectivity from frontend
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Backend runs on port 8000 without errors
2. Frontend runs on port 3000 without errors
3. Security scan passes (or acceptable warnings)
4. Unit tests pass
5. Application works locally end-to-end
6. Vercel deployment completes successfully
7. Render/Railway deployment completes successfully
8. Live application is accessible and functional
9. API endpoints respond correctly
10. Frontend can communicate with backend

tasks.title: Create deploy/ directory structure and move Docker files
tasks.description: Create the new deploy/ directory with docker/ and compose/ subdirectories, and move all Docker-related files from root and frontend locations to the new structure.
tasks.details:
1. Create directory structure: deploy/docker/, deploy/compose/, deploy/k8s/
2. Move Dockerfile.backend (root) to deploy/docker/Dockerfile.backend
3. Move frontend/Dockerfile.frontend to deploy/docker/Dockerfile.frontend
4. Move docker-compose.yml (root) to deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml
5. Create deploy/compose/docker-compose.gpu.yml for GPU variant based on docker-compose.yml
6. Update all path references in Docker files (e.g., COPY commands, WORKDIR paths) to reflect new project root relative locations
7. Update docker-compose files to reference Dockerfiles at their new locations (../docker/...)
8. Create placeholder .gitkeep in deploy/k8s/ for future use
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify directory structure matches PRD specification
2. Run 'docker build -f deploy/docker/Dockerfile.backend .' to ensure backend builds
3. Run 'docker build -f deploy/docker/Dockerfile.frontend ./frontend' to ensure frontend builds
4. Run 'docker-compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml config' to validate compose file syntax
5. Run 'docker-compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.gpu.yml config' to validate GPU variant

tasks.title: Restructure scripts/ directory into subdirectories
tasks.description: Reorganize the scripts/ directory by creating subdirectories (ci, dev, docs, security, validation) and moving existing scripts into their appropriate categories.
tasks.details:
1. Create subdirectories: scripts/ci/, scripts/dev/, scripts/docs/, scripts/security/, scripts/validation/
2. Move ci-related scripts to scripts/ci/: ci_parity.py, ci_full.py, compose_smoke.py, coverage_gate.py, security_local.py
3. Move development scripts to scripts/dev/: bootstrap_uv.py, start.py, start.sh, start.ps1, setup.sh, setup.ps1, run-ci-full.ps1, run-pre-commit.ps1, run-docker-cpu.ps1, run-docker-gpu.ps1, run-docker-gpu-internet.ps1, verify_ollama.ps1, sync_claude_mcp_from_trae.ps1
4. Move documentation scripts to scripts/docs/: export_openapi.py, generate_api_reference.py, update_api_reference_full.py
5. Move security scripts to scripts/security/: forbidden_tokens_check.py, security_scan.py
6. Move validation scripts to scripts/validation/: system_validate.py, validate_taskmaster.py
7. Review any remaining scripts in root scripts/ and categorize appropriately or remove if obsolete
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify directory tree matches PRD structure using 'tree scripts/' or similar
2. For each moved script, verify shebang lines and imports still work
3. Run 'python -m py_compile' on each Python script to check for syntax errors
4. Execute a representative script from each category (e.g., scripts/dev/start.py) to verify path resolution

tasks.title: Update .devcontainer configuration for FastAPI + Next.js
tasks.description: Update the .devcontainer/devcontainer.json file to reflect the new FastAPI backend and Next.js frontend stack, removing outdated Streamlit references.
tasks.details:
1. Update .devcontainer/devcontainer.json:
   - Change image to one supporting Python 3.11+ and Node.js
   - Update extensions: include Python, Prettier, ESLint, Docker extensions
   - Update forwardPorts: add 8000 (FastAPI), 3000 (Next.js), remove Streamlit ports
   - Update postCreateCommand to use uv for Python setup and npm/yarn for frontend
   - Configure workspaceFolder to point to project root
   - Add environment variables for development mode
2. Update VS Code settings in devcontainer for Python interpreter and formatting
3. Add healthcheck commands in lifecycle scripts to verify services are running
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Validate JSON syntax of devcontainer.json
2. Build the dev container: 'devcontainer build --workspace-folder .'
3. Reopen in container and verify:
   - Python 3.11+ is available
   - Node.js is available
   - uv is installed
   - Extensions are loaded
   - Ports 8000 and 3000 are forwarded
4. Run postCreateCommand manually to verify setup completes

tasks.title: Update security script references and remove broken wrapper
tasks.description: Update documentation and CI references to use scripts/security/local_scan.py as the canonical security scanner, and remove the broken security-scan.py wrapper.
tasks.details:
1. Remove root-level security-scan.py wrapper (already broken - imports non-existent module)
2. Update documentation references:
   - CLAUDE.md: Update security section to reference scripts/security/local_scan.py
   - README.md: Update security scan command
   - .taskmaster/docs/prd_refactoring.md: Update planned paths
3. Update CI workflow references if needed:
   - .github/workflows/ci.yml already uses correct paths (scripts/security/)
4. Verify local_scan.py works correctly:
   - Run 'python scripts/security/local_scan.py --help'
   - Run 'python scripts/security/local_scan.py --quick'
5. Update docstring in local_scan.py to reference correct path
6. No file rename needed - local_scan.py is already the canonical location
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify root security-scan.py is removed
2. Verify scripts/security/local_scan.py exists and runs
3. Run 'python scripts/security/local_scan.py --help' to verify it executes
4. Run 'python scripts/security/local_scan.py --quick' for quick scan
5. Check documentation for correct paths
6. Verify CI workflows reference correct paths

tasks.title: Update CI/CD workflows with new paths
tasks.description: Update .github/workflows/ci.yml and .github/workflows/semgrep.yml to use the new script paths and Docker file locations.
tasks.details:
1. Update .github/workflows/ci.yml:
   - Update script paths: scripts/xxx -> scripts/ci/xxx, scripts/dev/xxx, etc.
   - Update docker-compose paths: docker-compose.yml -> deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml
   - Update Dockerfile paths for build steps
   - Update security scan command to use scripts/security/local_scan.py
2. Update .github/workflows/semgrep.yml if it references any moved scripts
3. Verify all environment variables and secrets references remain valid
4. Ensure working-directory settings in workflow jobs are correct for new structure
5. Update any relative path references in workflow commands
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Validate YAML syntax for both workflow files
2. Dry-run workflow locally using 'act' or similar tool if available
3. Create a test branch and push to trigger CI workflow
4. Monitor GitHub Actions logs for path-related errors
5. Verify all workflow steps complete successfully

tasks.title: Create Makefile for common commands
tasks.description: Create a root-level Makefile with convenient targets for security scanning, testing, linting, docker operations, and running full CI locally.
tasks.details:
1. Create Makefile with the following targets:
   - make security: runs 'python scripts/security/local_scan.py'
   - make test: runs 'python -m pytest' or equivalent test command
   - make lint: runs 'ruff check .' and 'ruff format --check .'
   - make docker-up: runs 'docker-compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml up -d'
   - make docker-down: runs 'docker-compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml down'
   - make ci: runs full CI locally via scripts/dev/run-ci-full.ps1 or equivalent
   - make setup: runs scripts/dev/setup.sh (or .ps1 on Windows)
   - make dev: runs scripts/dev/start.py
2. Add .PHONY declaration for all targets
3. Include help target that displays all available commands
4. Add variables for common paths (DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE, SCRIPT_DIR, etc.)
5. Ensure Makefile is cross-platform compatible (or document Windows limitations)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run 'make help' to verify all targets are listed
2. Execute each target individually:
   - make security
   - make test
   - make lint
   - make docker-up (verify containers start)
   - make docker-down (verify containers stop)
   - make ci
3. Verify targets fail appropriately when dependencies are missing
4. Test Makefile on both Unix and Windows (with WSL or make equivalent)

tasks.title: Update documentation with new structure
tasks.description: Update README.md, CLAUDE.md, and any other documentation files to reflect the new project structure, script locations, and commands.
tasks.details:
1. Update README.md:
   - Update project structure diagram
   - Update all script path references (old -> new)
   - Update Docker commands to use new paths
   - Update development setup instructions
   - Add Makefile usage examples
2. Update CLAUDE.md:
   - Update script paths for AI assistant context
   - Reflect new directory structure in any path descriptions
   - Update workflow references
3. Update any inline documentation or comments in moved files
4. Update CONTRIBUTING.md if it exists with new command patterns
5. Search for references to old paths in other .md files and update accordingly
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Use grep to find remaining references to old paths: 'security-scan.py', 'scripts/ci_parity.py', 'Dockerfile.backend' at root
2. Verify all code examples in documentation are valid
3. Check that all internal links still work
4. Have another team member review documentation for clarity

tasks.title: Final validation and integration testing
tasks.description: Perform comprehensive validation to ensure all refactored paths work correctly, Docker containers build and run, and all scripts execute without errors.
tasks.details:
1. Run full CI pipeline to verify all changes work together
2. Test Docker workflows:
   - Build backend: 'docker build -f deploy/docker/Dockerfile.backend .'
   - Build frontend: 'docker build -f deploy/docker/Dockerfile.frontend ./frontend'
   - Start services: 'docker-compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml up -d'
   - Verify services are accessible on expected ports
3. Test all Makefile targets end-to-end
4. Run security scan: 'python scripts/security/local_scan.py' or 'make security'
5. Test dev container from fresh start:
   - Delete .devcontainer cache
   - Rebuild and reopen container
   - Verify all setup completes
6. Validate no broken imports or path references in any Python file
7. Create a smoke test that exercises key functionality
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Full CI/CD pipeline run with green status
2. All Makefile targets pass without errors
3. Docker containers start successfully and respond to health checks
4. All scripts in scripts/ execute without import errors
5. Dev container builds and setup completes within expected time
6. No broken links or missing file references found via automated checks
7. Create validation checklist and verify all items pass

tasks.title: Refactor repo for local PowerShell scripts and Docker
tasks.description: Fix port consistency, Docker configuration issues, update documentation, and add convenience scripts for easier local development.
tasks.details:
1. Fix port consistency:
   - Update scripts/launcher/start.py: change default ports from 8001/3001 to 8000/3000
   - Update deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml: change port mappings from 8001:8000 to 8000:8000 and 3001:3000 to 3000:3000
2. Fix Docker issues:
   - Add API_ACCESS_KEY default in docker-compose.yml: ${API_ACCESS_KEY:-dev-secret-key}
   - Fix frontend health check for Windows compatibility (use localhost instead of $(hostname -i))
   - Add environment variable documentation in docker-compose.yml comments
3. Update documentation:
   - Add scripts/ directory structure documentation to CLAUDE.md
   - Update all script path references (scripts/dev/ -> scripts/core/ or scripts/launcher/)
   - Update all port references to use 8000/3000 consistently
   - Update QUICKSTART.md with correct script examples
4. Add convenience scripts:
   - Create scripts/run.ps1 for easy local development
   - Create scripts/docker-up.ps1 for quick Docker start
5. Verify all changes work correctly
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run .\scripts\core\local\backend.ps1 - verify backend runs on port 8000
2. Run .\scripts\core\local\frontend.ps1 - verify frontend runs on port 3000
3. Run .\scripts\core\local\run.ps1 - verify both services start
4. Run .\scripts\core\docker\cpu.ps1 - verify Docker starts correctly
5. Run .\scripts\run.ps1 - verify convenience wrapper works
6. Run .\scripts\docker-up.ps1 - verify Docker wrapper works
7. Check all documentation for correct paths and ports

tasks.title: User Authentication System
tasks.description: Implement real authentication to replace the current simulated auth system. Users should be able to register, login, and manage their sessions securely.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - JWT Authentication:
   - Create User model with email, password_hash, verified, created_at
   - Implement /auth/register endpoint with email verification
   - Implement /auth/login endpoint returning JWT tokens
   - Implement /auth/logout endpoint for session invalidation
   - Implement /auth/refresh endpoint for token renewal
   - Implement /auth/password-reset endpoint

2. Backend - OAuth Providers:
   - Add Google OAuth integration
   - Add Apple OAuth integration (optional)
   - Create /auth/oauth/{provider} endpoints

3. Backend - Middleware:
   - Add JWT validation middleware
   - Add rate limiting for auth endpoints
   - Add CSRF protection

4. Frontend - Auth Pages:
   - Update /auth/login with real form submission
   - Update /auth/register with email verification flow
   - Add /auth/forgot-password page
   - Add /auth/reset-password page
   - Store JWT tokens securely (httpOnly cookies preferred)

5. Frontend - Auth State:
   - Create AuthContext for user state management
   - Add protected route wrapper
   - Add logout functionality
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for password hashing and JWT generation
2. Integration tests for all auth endpoints
3. Test OAuth flow with mock providers
4. Test token refresh and expiration
5. Test rate limiting on auth endpoints
6. E2E tests for login/register/logout flows

tasks.title: Saved Searches & Alerts
tasks.description: Allow users to save their search criteria and receive email alerts when new properties match their saved searches.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Saved Search Model:
   - Create SavedSearch model with user_id, filters, alert_frequency, is_active
   - Store filter criteria (price_range, location, property_type, rooms, etc.)

2. Backend - API Endpoints:
   - GET /saved-searches - List user's saved searches
   - POST /saved-searches - Create new saved search
   - PUT /saved-searches/{id} - Update saved search
   - DELETE /saved-searches/{id} - Delete saved search
   - PATCH /saved-searches/{id}/toggle - Pause/resume alerts

3. Backend - Alert System:
   - Background job to check for new matching properties
   - Email notification when matches found
   - Daily/weekly digest options
   - Rate limiting to prevent spam

4. Frontend - UI:
   - "Save Search" button on search results page
   - Saved searches management in settings
   - Alert frequency selector
   - Toggle for pausing alerts
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for saved search model and filters
2. Integration tests for CRUD operations
3. Test alert triggering with mock properties
4. Test email notification sending
5. E2E test for save/search/alert flow

tasks.title: Property Favorites/Watchlist
tasks.description: Implement a favorites/watchlist system where users can save individual properties for later review, organize them into collections, and add notes.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Favorites Model:
   - Create Favorite model with user_id, property_id, collection_id, notes, created_at
   - Create Collection model with user_id, name, description, is_default

2. Backend - API Endpoints:
   - GET /favorites - List user's favorites
   - POST /favorites - Add property to favorites
   - DELETE /favorites/{property_id} - Remove from favorites
   - PATCH /favorites/{property_id} - Update notes/collection
   - GET /collections - List collections
   - POST /collections - Create collection
   - PUT /collections/{id} - Update collection
   - DELETE /collections/{id} - Delete collection

3. Frontend - UI:
   - Heart icon on property cards to add/remove favorites
   - /favorites page with grid view of saved properties
   - Collection sidebar for organizing favorites
   - Notes editor for each saved property
   - Share watchlist via link (optional)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for favorites model
2. Integration tests for CRUD operations
3. Test collection management
4. Test notes functionality
5. E2E test for add/view/remove favorites

tasks.title: Price History & Trends
tasks.description: Implement price history tracking and trend analysis to show how property prices have changed over time.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Price History Model:
   - Create PriceSnapshot model with property_id, price, price_per_sqm, recorded_at
   - Background job to capture price snapshots periodically

2. Backend - API Endpoints:
   - GET /market/price-history/{property_id} - Get price history for a property
   - GET /market/trends - Get market trend data (avg prices by city/district)
   - GET /market/indicators - Get market indicators (rising/falling/stable)

3. Backend - Analytics:
   - Calculate price trends (1m, 3m, 6m, 1y)
   - Calculate market indicators
   - Price drop detection and alerts

4. Frontend - UI:
   - Price history chart component (line chart)
   - Trend indicators on property cards
   - Market trends dashboard
   - Price alert subscription
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for trend calculations
2. Integration tests for price history API
3. Test price snapshot capture
4. Test alert triggering on price drops
5. Verify chart rendering with sample data

tasks.title: Advanced Investment Analytics
tasks.description: Enhance the investment analysis tools with more advanced metrics including cash flow projections, tax implications, and risk assessment.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Enhanced Calculators:
   - Cash flow projection (1, 5, 10, 20 years)
   - Tax implications calculator (property tax, income tax deductions)
   - Appreciation scenarios (pessimistic -2%, realistic +3%, optimistic +7%)
   - Risk assessment score (0-100) based on market volatility

2. Backend - Portfolio Metrics:
   - Aggregate metrics for multiple properties
   - Diversification score
   - Overall portfolio risk

3. Frontend - Enhanced UI:
   - Cash flow projection chart
   - Scenario comparison table
   - Risk assessment visualization
   - Portfolio dashboard view
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for all calculator functions
2. Verify projection calculations
3. Test risk scoring algorithm
4. Verify chart data formatting
5. Compare results with known investment scenarios

tasks.title: Neighborhood Insights Enhancement
tasks.description: Enhance the neighborhood quality index with more detailed data including crime statistics, school ratings, and public transport accessibility.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Data Sources:
   - Integrate crime statistics API (if available for Poland)
   - Integrate school ratings/distance data
   - Add public transport accessibility score (GTFS data)
   - Add air quality index integration
   - Add noise level data

2. Backend - Enhanced Scoring:
   - Update NeighborhoodQualityIndexTool with new factors
   - Add weighted scoring configuration
   - Add historical comparison

3. Frontend - Enhanced UI:
   - Detailed neighborhood breakdown (crime, schools, transport)
   - Comparison with city average
   - Interactive map with POI markers
   - "What's nearby" section
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for each scoring factor
2. Integration tests with external APIs
3. Verify scoring calculations
4. Test fallback when data unavailable
5. Verify map marker rendering

tasks.title: Multi-language UI
tasks.description: Implement full internationalization (i18n) for the UI, supporting Polish (primary market), English, and Russian languages.
tasks.details:

tasks.title: Rent vs Buy Calculator
tasks.description: Create a calculator that compares the financial implications of renting vs buying a property over time.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Calculator:
   - Monthly rent vs mortgage payment comparison
   - Total cost calculation over 5, 10, 15, 30 years
   - Break-even point calculation (when buying becomes cheaper)
   - Opportunity cost of down payment (if invested instead)
   - Tax benefits of ownership (deductions)
   - Maintenance and repair costs
   - Property appreciation consideration

2. API Endpoint:
   - POST /tools/rent-vs-buy - Calculate comparison

3. Frontend - UI:
   - Rent vs Buy calculator form
   - Side-by-side comparison chart
   - Break-even timeline visualization
   - Sensitivity analysis (what-if scenarios)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for all calculations
2. Verify break-even calculation
3. Test with various input scenarios
4. Compare with online calculators for validation
5. E2E test for calculator flow

tasks.title: Document Management
tasks.description: Implement a document management system where users can upload, store, and organize property-related documents such as contracts, inspection reports, and photos.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Document Model:
   - Create Document model with user_id, property_id, filename, file_type, category, tags, expiry_date
   - Support file types: PDF, DOC, DOCX, JPG, PNG

2. Backend - Storage:
   - Configure file storage (local S3-compatible or cloud storage)
   - Implement secure file upload/download
   - Add file size limits (10MB max)
   - Add virus scanning (optional)

3. Backend - API Endpoints:
   - POST /documents - Upload document
   - GET /documents - List documents
   - GET /documents/{id} - Download document
   - DELETE /documents/{id} - Delete document
   - PATCH /documents/{id} - Update metadata

4. Backend - OCR (optional):
   - Extract text from PDFs/images
   - Make documents searchable

5. Frontend - UI:
   - Document upload with drag-drop
   - Document list with filters
   - Document preview (PDF viewer)
   - Expiry reminders
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for document model
2. Integration tests for upload/download
3. Test file type validation
4. Test file size limits
5. Test document categorization
6. Verify secure file access

4. Mobile Optimization:

5. Files to create:

tasks.title: Agent/Broker Integration
tasks.description: Integrate real estate agent/broker functionality to connect users with professionals for property inquiries, viewing appointments, and listing management.
tasks.details:
1. Backend - Agent Model:
   - Create Agent model with name, email, phone, agency, rating, specialties, service_areas

2. Backend - API Endpoints:
   - GET /agents - List agents (with filters by area, specialty)
   - GET /agents/{id} - Get agent details
   - POST /agents/{id}/contact - Send inquiry to agent
   - POST /agents/{id}/schedule-viewing - Request viewing appointment
   - GET /agents/{id}/listings - Get agent's listings

3. Backend - Agent Dashboard (optional):
   - Agent registration/login
   - Manage listings
   - View inquiries
   - Schedule management

4. Frontend - UI:
   - Agent directory page with search/filters
   - Agent profile page with ratings/reviews
   - Contact form on property pages
   - Viewing appointment scheduler
   - Commission calculator tool
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for agent model
2. Integration tests for all endpoints
3. Test contact form submission
4. Test appointment scheduling
5. Verify agent search/filtering
6. E2E test for agent contact flow

tasks.title: Frontend Auth Integration
tasks.description: Implement frontend authentication integration with JWT backend. Connect login/register pages to real API endpoints, create AuthContext for state management, and add protected routes.
tasks.details:
This task depends on Task #35 (User Authentication System - Backend) which provides the JWT auth endpoints.

1. Auth Context:
   - Create AuthContext.tsx for user state management
   - Create useAuth hook for easy access
   - Handle loading states and error handling
   - Store CSRF token from cookie

2. Auth API Functions:
   - Create lib/auth.ts with API functions
   - login(email, password)
   - register(email, password, name)
   - logout()
   - getCurrentUser()
   - forgotPassword(email)
   - resetPassword(token, password)
   - getGoogleOAuthUrl()

3. Auth Pages Refactor:
   - Update /auth/login with real API calls and OAuth button
   - Update /auth/register with real API calls
   - Create /auth/forgot-password page
   - Create /auth/reset-password page
   - Create /auth/verify-email page
   - Create /auth/oauth/callback page

4. Protected Routes:
   - Create middleware.ts for route protection
   - Check for auth cookies
   - Redirect unauthenticated users to /auth/login
   - Allow public routes: /auth/*, /api/*, /health

5. UI Components:
   - Create LoginForm.tsx component
   - Create RegisterForm.tsx component
   - Create OAuthButtons.tsx component
   - Create UserMenu.tsx component for header

6. Integration:
   - Update header to show user menu when authenticated
   - Add logout functionality
   - Handle token refresh on 401 responses
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Component tests for AuthContext
2. Form validation tests
3. Protected route tests
4. E2E tests for login/register/logout flows
5. Test OAuth flow (mocked)

tasks.title: Auth Security Hardening
tasks.description: Add security hardening measures for the authentication system including CSRF protection, rate limiting on auth endpoints, and audit logging integration.
tasks.details:
This task depends on Task #35 (User Authentication System - Backend) which provides the JWT auth endpoints.

1. CSRF Protection:
   - Create api/middleware/csrf.py
   - Implement double-submit cookie pattern
   - Read CSRF token from cookie
   - Validate against X-CSRF-Token header
   - Skip for GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests
   - Skip for routes using Authorization header (API clients)

2. Rate Limiting on Auth Endpoints:
   - Login: 5 attempts per minute per IP
   - Register: 3 per minute per IP
   - Password reset: 3 per minute per IP
   - Email verification resend: 3 per minute per IP
   - Use existing rate limiter from middleware/observability.py

3. Audit Logging Integration:
   - Log all auth events (login, logout, register)
   - Log failed login attempts
   - Log password reset requests
   - Integrate with existing audit.py system

4. RBAC Integration:
   - Map JWT roles to existing Role enum in rbac.py
   - Extend UserContext with user_id from JWT
   - Add get_user_context_from_jwt function

5. Security Headers:
   - Ensure auth cookies have Secure flag in production
   - Verify SameSite=Strict is set
   - Add rate limit headers to responses

6. Additional Security:
   - Add account lockout after 5 failed login attempts
   - Add breached password checking (optional, haveibeenpwned API)
   - Add password strength validation on server side
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for CSRF middleware
2. Integration tests for rate limiting
3. Test account lockout mechanism
4. Security penetration tests
5. Test audit logging captures all auth events
